channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Why we still need {admiral} in an age of AI
item.description: There is a version of the AI-in-pharma story that goes like this: LLMs are trained on vast amounts of R code, so they can write ADaM programs on demand. Packages like admiral {admiral} become optional — a style preference rather than a requi... Continue readi…

item.title: As anthropic suspends access to new models, india debates its AI future | techcrunch
item.description: As Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates its AI future | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Meta reportedly moves to unwind $2b manus deal after beijing's demand | techcrunch
item.description: Meta starts dismantling its $2 billion Manus acquisition after Beijing ordered the deal reversed.

item.title: Openai says it's engaging 'constructively' with state ags about concerns
item.description: The Wall Street Journal reported that a coalition of state attorneys general have opened an investigation into OpenAI.

item.title: Anthropic's safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
item.description: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people," the company wrote in a blog post.

item.title: Anthropic says it’s taking claude fable 5 offline to comply with US government order
item.description: “The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or ‘jailbreaking’ Fable 5,” the company said in a blog post.

item.title: A better way to manage all your screenshots
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 132, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, happy soccer, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) This week, I've been preparing for a month …

item.title: Here’s how AI agents can protect ev chargers
item.description: An AI agent system proposed by researchers in Spain promises to prevent energy theft and damage to EV chargers, as well as the critical energy infrastructure that powers them.

item.title: Anthropic pre-ipo market falls after US directive forces model shutdown
item.description: Anthropic says it disabled Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after a US government export-control directive, while pre-IPO-linked trading weakened.

item.title: Evicore by evernorth rated #1 as prior authorization becomes a top health plan technology acquisition priority
item.description: eviCore by Evernorth Rated #1 as Prior Authorization Becomes a Top Health Plan Technology Acquisition Prioritymorningstar.com

item.title: Eli lilly’s AI data push meets new obesity rivalry from pfizer
item.description: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has invested in Abridge, an AI-enabled clinical documentation company, marking a move into AI supported clinical research and real-world data capture. The partnership is aimed at improving trial enrollment and real-world evidence generati…

item.title: This week’s awesome tech stories from around the web (through june 13)
item.description: Every week, we scour the web for important, insightful, and fascinating stories in science and technology. The post This Week’s Awesome Tech Stories From Around the Web (Through June 13) appeared first on SingularityHub.

item.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the white house’s anthropic fable ban
item.description: According to the Wall Street Journal, the export control directive that led to Anthropic cutting off access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 was triggered in part by cybersecurity research from Amazon and conversations between CEO Andy Jassy and the White House. Accor…

item.title: Amazon CEO reportedly raised anthropic model concerns before government crackdown | techcrunch
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy may have been the source of security concerns that led Anthropic to cut off worldwide access to two models on Friday.

item.title: Openai faces investigation from state attorneys general | techcrunch
item.description: It's not clear which states are involved, but they're asking about everything from OpenAI's ad policies to its handling of health data.

item.title: World cup AI predictor now lets users ask daft what-ifs
item.description: Spoiler: It doesn't end well for Team Register

item.title: Anthropic cuts off fable 5 and mythos 5 access following government order
item.description: On Friday evening, the government ordered Anthropic to block access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nations, both inside and outside the US, due to national security concerns. That order included employees of Anthropic. To meet those demands, the comp…

item.title: The fcc wants to kill burner phones
item.description: Plus: AI bug hunting fuels Microsoft’s biggest-ever Patch Tuesday, ShinyHunters ransomware gang exploits an Oracle zero-day, and more.

item.title: Kpmg pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations | techcrunch
item.description: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Towards AI-augmented decision making in psychiatry
item.description: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 12 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01256-2 Psychiatric disorders are heterogeneous, and care depends on interpreting unstructured longitudinal narratives, creating variability that hinders standardization. A study now shows that a psychiatry-specific

item.title: Atomic-level protein–ligand recognition with pbcnet2.0 for probe discovery
item.description: Protein–ligand binding affinity prediction is important for the development and optimization of molecular probes. PBCNet2.0 predicts relative affinity changes induced by ligand modifications (for example, functional groups and stereochemistry) and shows an em…

item.title: Stocks to watch today, 12 june: dabur india, vedanta, gng electronics, infosys, hexagon nutrition, irctc, genxai analytics, state bank of india (sbi), lenskart solutions, sagility
item.description: Stocks To Watch Today, 12 June: Dabur India, Vedanta, GNG Electronics, Infosys, Hexagon Nutrition, IRCTC, GenXAI Analytics, State Bank of India (SBI), Lenskart Solutions, Sagilitysundayguardianlive.com

item.title: Holistic AI adoption: the key to unlocking enterprise value
item.description: While AI adoption is accelerating, 77% or organizations still report no meaningful impact on overall revenue.

item.title: Designing enterprise AI guardrails: security, compliance, and risk management
item.description: Design robust enterprise AI guardrails for security, compliance, and risk management. Ensure safe, responsible, and trustworthy AI integration.

item.title: Nin fraudsters risk seven-year jail, icpc warns
item.description: ICPC warns NIMC enrolment partners that involvement in NIN fraud, including registering non-Nigerians, could lead to a seven-year prison sentence. Read More: https://punchng.com/nin-fraudsters-risk-seven-year-jail-icpc-warns/

item.title: Proteomic prediction of apol1-associated kidney disease
item.description: Nature Genetics - Proteomic prediction of APOL1-associated kidney disease

item.title: General-purpose large language models outperform specialized clinical AI tools on medical benchmarks
item.description: In an independent evaluation, frontier large language models outperformed specialized clinical artificial intelligence tools on medical knowledge, clinician alignment and real-world clinical queries.

item.title: Comparative analysis of progression milestones across Parkinson's disease clinical, pathological, and data-driven subtypes: a 10-year follow-up
item.description: This 10-year longitudinal study compares disease progression milestones across three classification approaches for Parkinson's disease: clinical subtypes, pathological subtypes, and data-driven subtypes. The analysis evaluates how different disease categorization methods predict progression patterns

item.title: FDA Clears First Over-the-Counter Continuous Glucose Monitor for Children
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today cleared for marketing the first over-the-counter (OTC) continuous glucose monitor (CGM) for children, Dexcom Inc.’s Stelo Glucose Biosensor System, an integrated CGM (iCGM) indicated for people two years of age and older who do not use insulin.

item.title: AI outpaces trust in healthcare as patients and doctors struggle to keep up, 2026 study finds
item.description: The 2026 Future Health Index found AI is transforming clinical care, but trust gaps among doctors and patients are slowing adoption and threatening patient safety.

item.title: Eli lilly's $2.75 billion AI bet on anti-aging drugs signals a major shift in pharmaceutical priorities
item.description: Eli Lilly's $2.75B AI deal with Insilico Medicine targets age-related diseases. How AI is transforming longevity drug discovery and what it means for patients.

item.title: Networknewsaudio announces audio press release (apr) discussing emerging AI platform companies in the technology ecosystem
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas, June 12, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — via IBN — NetworkNewsAudio announces the Audio Press Release (APR) titled “A Next-Gen Intelligence Platform Operating at the Intersection of AI, Defense Technology, and Quantum Cybersecurity,” featuring Redwood …

item.title: The gender data gap and the need for representation in AI
item.description: What is often overlooked is that mitigating AI gender bias starts with the data itself.

item.title: AI update, june 12, 2026: AI news and views from the past week
item.description: Catch up on select AI news and developments since Friday, June 5. Stay in the know. Read the full article at MarketingProfs

item.title: Drug-induced gastric motility disorders: a disproportionality analysis from the faers and cvard databases
item.description: Background Delayed gastric emptying and gastroesophageal reflux represent critical yet frequently underrecognized complications in hospitalized patients, particularly in the context of polypharmacy. While multiple medication classes have been implicated in di…

item.title: FDA clears first over-the-counter continuous glucose monitor for children
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today cleared for marketing the first over-the-counter (OTC) continuous glucose monitor (CGM) for children, Dexcom Inc.’s Stelo Glucose Biosensor System, an integrated CGM (iCGM) indicated for people two years of age and …

item.title: 6 beauty product ingredients banned in the EU but legal in the US
item.description: Formaldehyde and other known carcinogens are among ingredients that can still legally be included in American cosmetics

item.title: Martin resch: how cfos can use AI to improve visibility, control, and savings
item.description: Martin Resch, President and Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Cass Information Systems, has spent his career building the infrastructure that fixes that foundation, and his view on where AI belongs in that work is precise. "LLMs can identify and highlight issu…

item.title: Ten simple rules for executing an inherited research plan in computational biology
item.description: Trainees in computational biology frequently inherit research plans whose aims, datasets, analytical strategies, and technical constraints were defined before their arrival. These plans often emerge from grants, collaborations, legacy codebases, shared high-p…

item.title: A new method for augmenting short time series, with application to pain events in sickle cell disease
item.description: Author summary When studying health conditions like sickle cell disease, we often face a frustrating challenge: individual patient datasets are too small or sparse to draw reliable conclusions. We developed a method to overcome this by combining data from mul…

item.title: Altered brain–behavior coupling during inhibitory control in ankylosing spondylitis: erp evidence from nogo-p3 component
item.description: Background Cognitive dysfunction has increasingly been recognized in Ankylosing Spondylitis (AS), yet the neural mechanisms underlying inhibitory control in this population remain insufficiently characterized. Rather than reflecting a simple global deficit, c…

item.title: Dynamic analysis of critical maternal complications in tertiary hospitals in wuxi: a study based on four years of monitoring data
item.description: Background Severe maternal morbidity (SMM) is a significant public health concern. This study analyzed the incidence, trends, causes, and pregnancy outcomes of SMM in Wuxi to inform future clinical and public health strategies. Methods A retrospective analy…

item.title: How gen AI is disrupting b2b buying decisions
item.description: Organizations built around sales relationships and channel control must adapt to new types of recommendation systems.

item.title: Elon musk is the world's first trillionaire
item.description: SpaceX’s stock market debut has thrust the richest man in the universe into an unexplored frontier of wealth.

item.title: China didn't make people hate data centers
item.description: GOP lawmakers, tech investors, and even OpenAI have tied the anti-data center movement in the US to Chinese interference. Experts say it’s much more complicated than that.

item.title: A trillion dollars is a stupid amount of money
item.description: Elon Musk is now officially the world's first trillionaire. That is a colossal amount of wealth (and by proxy, power) for one individual to have. Its scale - a thousand times more than a billion - is difficult to fathom for those of us who aren't among the 3,…

item.title: Elon musk is the world’s first trillionaire
item.description: Elon Musk's net worth has passed the trillion-dollar mark after SpaceX's IPO. His net worth, which was hovering around $800 billion before the IPO, includes the value of his 4.8 billion shares in SpaceX, along with his wealth from his other companies, like Te…

item.title: Spacex’s massive ipo: all the latest news
item.description: SpaceX’s IPO on Friday allows the public to buy shares of the combined rocket, AI, and social media company for the first time, and is raising enough money to likely make Elon Musk the first trillionaire. He’ll have more wealth, on paper at least, than the e…

item.title: Spacex is now public
item.description: SpaceX is now a publicly traded company. In one of the most highly anticipated and controversial public offerings of all time, the rocket venture helmed by Elon Musk started trading on Nasdaq on June 12th at the take-it-or-leave-it price of $135-per-share - t…

item.title: Siri won’t be your AI girlfriend
item.description: Our early testing has already shown that Siri AI knows when to shut up, and that's very much by design. In an interview with Mostly Human, Craig Federighi said Apple's new Siri won't act all sycophantic like chatbots made by OpenAI, Google, and others. "As yo…
